The current observation shows that absorbance (A) is directly proportional to both molar_absorptivity (ε) and path_length (l), and inversely proportional to transmittance (T). Since we have already increased both ε and l, it is reasonable to assume that absorbance can be calculated using the Beer-Lambert Law: A = εcl, where c is the concentration of the solute.
